summary:
The patient has Eisenmenger’s syndrome because among these cardiovascular disorders, its acute complications such as profound hypoxia and recurrent heart failures pose an extremely high risk for maternal morbidity and mortality specifically related to compromised lung perfusion and oxygen exchange capabilities under physiological stresses associated with childbirth processes.
The primary reason behind higher maternal deaths linked closely with Eisenmengers' disease stems from unmanageable hemodynamic instability coupled with irreversible damage caused by persistent elevated pressures within lungs making recovery nearly impossible even post-delivery phase when normal physiology resumes back towards baseline levels expected outside gestational period context provided here.
